lines: Keziah Fullo wrote names where a road clerk would have written numbers. Candle grease stiffens the page edges.
The first leaf begins after the Stilling. Keziah crossed out the office heading, REMAINS RECEIVED, and wrote NAMES KEPT.
Twelve marks run down the inside cover. The last was pressed so hard that the paper split.
I asked Aaron if the stone still hurt. Two cracks came through the chair beneath my hand, and I did not ask him again.
Susanna heard the bell while the rope was still in my fist. She struck her teeth until I cut out the clapper.
Jeremiah knew my step. The ash moved at his feet before I touched the gate.
Margin cuts beside Monica show a broken circle. Thomas is marked by opened ribs; Susanna by the bell mouth.
A final page names Sapphira Rufa. Keziah wrote that the wardens took him below the washing house because he turned north before Hallowfen's bells sounded.

effects.log: Keziah kept twelve names. Only eleven remain in the graveyard.

choices.label: Set Keziah's record beside the docket

questUpdate.log: Quest updated: find Sapphira Rufa and decide what remains.

effects.log: Names and subject numbers join. Sapphira answered Hallowfen before the road bells rang.

lines: The forced stone tore several leaves from the binding. Keziah's cramped hand survives along the inner margins.
I asked Aaron if the stone still hurt. Two cracks came through the chair beneath my hand, and I did not ask him again.
Susanna heard the bell while the rope was still in my fist. She struck her teeth until I cut out the clapper.
Jeremiah knew my step. The ash moved at his feet before I touched the gate.
One inner margin survives intact. Monica bears a broken circle, Thomas opened ribs, and Susanna the bell mouth.
Sapphira Rufa's page is split, but the last sentence remains: the wardens took him below after he turned north before the Hallowfen bells.

effects.log: The damaged register still keeps twelve names.

choices.label: Set the torn record beside the docket

questUpdate.log: Quest updated: find Sapphira Rufa and decide what remains.

effects.log: Enough survives to join names with numbers. Sapphira answered Hallowfen before the road bells rang.

lines: Keziah's book keeps twelve names. Sapphira Rufa is the only one without a place in the graveyard.
The removed clapper lies beside the entry where Susanna heard a bell that had not yet moved.
